__comment_why_this_file: host.json is REQUIRED by Azure Functions — the runtime refuses to start without it.
It configures behaviour that applies to ALL functions in this app (we only have one).
This is NOT where you put secrets or connection strings — that is local.settings.json.
__comment_version: version 2.0 means we are using the Azure Functions v2 programming model,
which is what our function_app.py is written for (the @app.route decorator style).
Never change this to 1.0 — the code would break.

__comment_logging: Controls how logs are sent to Azure Application Insights when deployed.
Locally, logs just print to your terminal regardless of this setting.

__comment_sampling: Sampling means Azure won't log EVERY single request — only a percentage.
This keeps Application Insights costs low for high-traffic functions.
For a class project this barely matters, but it's the correct default.
